commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3f992a0b55b0314d16a84e14a4e9bac9e14a04da)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes shared auto-reply delivery so streaming-off block replies send text-only chunks directly, then suppresses final plain assistant text when direct block replies were sent.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +123. Total +154 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main flushes block replies before tools, but streaming-off text-only blocks are not direct-sent. I did not run a live transport reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to fix the suppression bug and provide real transport proof; automation cannot supply the external PR author's real-environment proof by itself.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): direct-send text-only block replies when streaming off (#57225)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

When block streaming is disabled (the Discord, Telegram, msteams, IRC, Mattermost, Synology Chat, NextCloud Talk, Slack Block Kit, etc. default), the shared block-reply delivery handler in `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts` only sent media-bearing payloads directly and silently dropped text-only blocks. `flushBlockReplyBuffer` runs before every tool execution (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts:1044`) and at message_end, but the text it emitted there reached `createBlockReplyDeliveryHandler` and hit the `else if (blockHasNonTextContent)` guard â€” text-only never made it out. Tool results, in contrast, deliver immediately through `onToolResult` (serialized via `toolResultChain` at `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts:2489-2513`). The net effect for a sequence like `text1 â†’ tool_call â†’ text2 â†’ tool_call â†’ text3 â†’ tool_call â†’ final_summary` was: three tool outputs first, then one combined final reply â€” losing the live-typed interleaving.

Why does this matter now?

The ClawSweeper triage on #57225 (`clawsweeper:source-repro`) traced this to `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts` and noted the recent "await block-reply flush before tool execution starts" fix (`99d854db82`) only awaited replies that were already sent â€” it did not start sending the buffered text. Discord users see this most because Discord's bundled config leaves block streaming off; the same regression exists on every channel sharing the streaming-off + tool-interleave path.

What is the intended outcome?

- Text segments emitted between tool calls land in the user's channel in production order, just before each tool's output.
- The final assistant reply no longer re-delivers the same text the user already saw chunk-by-chunk.
- Pure text turns with no tool calls still arrive as one cohesive payload (streaming-off + no chunker = single buffered emit), so the streaming-off UX promise is preserved.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to channel-specific dispatchers (Discord, Telegram, etc.). The fix is purely in `src/auto-reply/reply`.
- No changes to the `blockStreaming` default or any channel config.
- No rewrite of the block-streaming pipeline; the streaming-enabled path is untouched.

What does success look like?

- New `reply-delivery.test.ts` cases prove text-only blocks are direct-sent and each segment is tracked in `directlySentBlockKeys` when streaming is off.
- New `agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` cases prove the canonical assistant text payload is suppressed once any block was directly delivered, while errors and media payloads still pass.
- Existing tests around block streaming, dedup, and reply-delivery continue to pass.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The two-part fix is coordinated: `reply-delivery.ts` direct-sends text-only blocks; `agent-runner-payloads.ts` suppresses the duplicate canonical text. Either change in isolation would either drop text or duplicate it.
- The new `isPlainAssistantAnswerPayload` predicate is the survivor surface â€” please sanity-check the metadata fields (`sourceReplyTranscriptMirror`, `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression`) and the `isError | isReasoning | isFallbackNotice | presentation | interactive | channelData | hasMedia` exclusions.
- Sibling-surface: this is a shared-layer fix; every channel inheriting the streaming-off default benefits. No channel-specific dispatcher needed code changes. PR-side this is the desired outcome â€” please confirm that's the maintainer preference rather than gating the fix behind a Discord-only flag.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #57225

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #2451 (Tyler Yust's original "send text between tool calls" fix in `agent-runner-execution.ts`, which had the same intent for the inline-handler path before the `reply-delivery.ts` extraction; the streaming-off direct-send branch was lost in a later refactor â€” `eefb2f8fb3` extracted `reply-delivery.ts`; `24032dcc0e` and `18c98316f7` reshaped the text/media handling and ended up only delivering blocks with non-text content when streaming is off).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No â€” sourced from the ClawSweeper triage on issue #57225 (labels `P1`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `impact:message-loss`).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Discord (and any streaming-off channel) delivers `text â†’ tool â†’ text â†’ tool â†’ text â†’ final` as `tool, tool, tool, combined_text`. After the fix it delivers in production order â€” each text segment direct-sends, each tool result follows immediately, and the canonical assistant answer payload is suppressed so the live chunks are not re-delivered as one combined reply.
- Real environment tested: Source-level repro + new automated tests in `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts`. The fix region was identified by ClawSweeper (`clawsweeper:source-repro`).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts` â†’ 14 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` â†’ 42 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts` â†’ 146 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.final-media-runreplyagent.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.block-streaming.test.ts` â†’ 3 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.calls-onblockreplyflush-before-tool-execution-start-preserve.test.ts` â†’ 10 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.suppresses-message-end-block-replies-message-tool.test.ts` â†’ 16 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts` â†’ 22 passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/queue.dedupe.test.ts` â†’ 8 passed.
  - `pnpm tsgo:core` â†’ exit 0 (no errors).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `reply-delivery.test.ts` adds `sends text-only block replies directly when block streaming is disabled (preserves tool-call interleaving)` and `delivers interleaved text segments and tracks each as a separately-sent block when streaming is disabled` â€” they assert that calling the delivery handler with text-only payloads triggers `onBlockReply` (the channel send) and grows `directlySentBlockKeys` by one entry per chunk.
  - `agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` adds `suppresses canonical assistant text payload when block chunks were directly delivered (#57225)`, `keeps error payloads when block chunks were directly delivered (#57225)`, and `keeps media-bearing payloads when block chunks were directly delivered (#57225)` â€” they prove the dedup drops the joined-final-text payload while leaving errors and media intact.
- Observed result after fix: Text segments and tool outputs interleave in production order at the shared auto-reply layer. Errors and media still pass through the final delivery. No duplicate text in the channel.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord bot end-to-end (issue is `clawsweeper:source-repro`, covering the source-level reproduction). I also did not exercise live Telegram / msteams / Slack / IRC / Mattermost end-to-end â€” the fix lives in the shared layer and all those channels read `blockStreamingEnabled` from the same config, so the same behavior change applies; sibling-surface coverage would benefit from a follow-up live test if any channel maintainer wants it.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Worktree was hydrated by the commit hook's `pnpm install`; broader Crabbox / Testbox proof was not run.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- All the per-file `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` runs listed above (211 tests across the touched and adjacent files passed).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (typecheck clean).

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts`: rewrote the obsolete `keeps text-only block replies buffered when block streaming is disabled` regression-guarding test (which locked in the buggy behavior) into two new tests that assert direct delivery and per-chunk key tracking.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts`: added three tests for the new dedup branch â€” canonical assistant text is suppressed when chunks were directly delivered, error payloads still pass, and media-bearing payloads still pass.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The `reply-delivery.test.ts` case at line 169 explicitly asserted the buggy behavior (`expect(onBlockReply).not.toHaveBeenCalled()` for text-only with streaming off). It was the regression-guarding lock that prevented the original Tyler Yust intent from being restored. It is now flipped to assert the fixed behavior.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A â€” tests are added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Channels using `blockStreaming=false` (Discord, Telegram, msteams, IRC, Mattermost, Synology Chat, NextCloud Talk, Slack Block Kit, etc.) will now stream block-reply chunks live during tool-interleaved runs instead of batching them into one final reply. Pure-text turns are unchanged because with no chunker the whole buffer flushes as a single block.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. No config keys, defaults, env vars, or migration surfaces are touched.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Final-payload dedup. The new `isPlainAssistantAnswerPayload` predicate suppresses plain assistant-text payloads when any block was already streamed directly. If the predicate is too broad it would swallow user-facing replies; if too narrow it would duplicate text.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The predicate only fires when `params.directlySentBlockKeys?.size > 0` and `blockStreamingEnabled === false` â€” turns that delivered no chunks (no tools, message-tool-only runs, source-reply mirror runs) keep the original behavior.
- Excludes `isError`, `isReasoning`, `isFallbackNotice`, `presentation`, `interactive`, `channelData`, `sourceReplyTranscriptMirror`, `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression`, and any payload with media â€” so errors, warnings, source-reply mirrors, presentations, interactive blocks, and media payloads still pass.
- Exact-key dedup still runs first; the plain-text branch is a fallback for the canonical-final-answer case where the key differs because the final text joins per-chunk text segments.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review. The fix is bounded to four files in `src/auto-reply/reply/`, no channel-specific code paths are touched, and the sibling-surface check is folded into the PR body (this is a shared-layer fix, intentionally not Discord-only).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Maintainer call on whether the sibling-surface behavior change for Telegram, msteams, Slack Block Kit, IRC, Mattermost, and the rest of the `blockStreaming=false` channels is desired in the same PR or should be gated channel-by-channel.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet; this is the first push.
